data.narrative:
Kepler Communications is a Series C-stage space and aerospace company that has raised $200M+ to date. The valuation assessment draws on a comparable universe of five same-sector, same-stage private peers (Sierra Space, Varda Space Industries, Skyryse, Isar Aerospace, Loft Orbital) and five public space sector benchmarks (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL).

Two triangulation methods are applicable. The stage-progression method, using total capital raised ($200M) as a valuation proxy and applying a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical for Series C advancement, implies a valuation of $340M. The peer-median method across five same-sector, same-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $1.1B, with a range from $1.0B to $8.0B. Public-comp anchoring using the sector's median price-to-sales multiple of 42.9x cannot be applied due to the absence of disclosed revenue data. The triangulated analysis across these two methods supports a valuation range of $289M (low) to $1.3B (high), with a median of $745M. Key uncertainty centers on revenue scale and growth trajectory relative to peer cohorts; the wide dispersion in peer valuations ($1.0B–$8.0B) reflects heterogeneous business models and market positions within the space sector.
